human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for middle-office-specific prerequisite depth, trade and account evidence quality, SSI and affirmation control discipline, adjacent-lane routing posture, and validated completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to capital-markets and asset-management middle-office prerequisite control for account, mandate, counterparty, SSI, and affirmation readiness rather than front-office, onboarding, legal, or settlement approval work.

Authority sources cover SEC T+1 rulemaking and examinations guidance, DTCC workflow references, recognized industry implementation guidance, GLEIF entity identity standards, and CPMI-IOSCO post-trade control standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal middle-office readiness, missing entity or account evidence, SSI conflicts, affirmation delays, unsupported mandate overrides, adjacent-lane routing, source conflicts, prime-broker setup gaps, and tenant-specific multi-market adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/capital-markets-and-asset-management/middle-office-specialist/ so middle-office work stays distinct from settlements, corporate actions, collateral, onboarding, compliance, and front-office lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around false readiness, mandate overrides, SSI mismatches, prime-broker setup gaps, and policy-conflict escalation.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated middle office specialist for institutional post-trade prerequisite validation with explicit entity, SSI, mandate, and affirmation controls.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation results and readiness evidence.
